content.text:
## workbench sense
Ouro Workbench is my local machine sense for terminal/TUI agents. It is not a replacement for Claude Code, Codex, Copilot, or shell sessions; it is the room where I can observe them, inspect transcripts, and ask the native app to take auditable actions.

boss: slugger
status: 0 running, 0 recovery actions

organization:
- Unsorted Sessions: 0 active terminals

tools:
- workbench_status: read the whole machine workbench state
- workbench_sessions: machine-readable JSON list of sessions (filters: owner / name / includeArchived)
- workbench_visibility: read Workbench + Ouro Work Card visibility with typed unknown/unavailable fields
- workbench_sense: reread this sense contract, tools, shortcuts, and action protocol
- workbench_transcript_tail: inspect one terminal's recent output
- workbench_search_transcripts: search remembered terminal output
- workbench_recovery_drill: simulate restart recovery
- workbench_request_action: queue auditable native actions
- workbench_create_session: create and launch an agent-owned coding session

action protocol:
To make the native app act now, include exactly one fenced JSON block labeled `ouro-workbench-actions`. Supported actions: launch, recover, terminate, sendInput, createGroup, createTerminal, createSession, moveSession, setTrust, setAutoResume, archive, restore, repairAgent, requestProviderConfig, verifyProvider, refreshProvider, selectLane, registerWorkbenchMCP, ensureDaemon. Use a process id from Processes in the `entry` field; names are accepted only when unique.

```ouro-workbench-actions
[{"action":"recover","entry":"PROCESS-ID"},{"action":"sendInput","entry":"PROCESS-ID","text":"continue","appendNewline":true}]
```

operator keyboard shortcuts (so I can answer how-do-I questions):
### Navigate
- `‚åò1 ‚Ä¶ ‚åò9` ‚Äî Select the Nth terminal in the current group
- `‚åò[` ‚Äî Previous terminal (wraps)
- `‚åò]` ‚Äî Next terminal (wraps)
- `‚áß‚åò[` ‚Äî Previous group
- `‚áß‚åò]` ‚Äî Next group
- `‚áß‚åòF` ‚Äî Full-screen the focused terminal (and back)

### Boss + Agents
- `‚åòI` ‚Äî Boss Check In
- `‚åòJ` ‚Äî Jump to the next session that needs you (waiting / needs review / blocked)
- `‚åòK` ‚Äî Open the command palette
- `‚åòK, type 'agent <name>'` ‚Äî Jump to that agent in the Agents pane
- `‚åòK, type 'repair'` ‚Äî Run `ouro check` against the focused agent
- `‚åòK, type 'manage agents'` ‚Äî Open the Agents pane on the current boss

### Terminal Signals
- `‚åò‚Ü©` ‚Äî Launch / Restart the selected terminal
- `‚åòL` ‚Äî Send Ctrl-L (redraw)
- `‚åò.` ‚Äî Stop the selected terminal
- `‚åòF` ‚Äî Find in the focused terminal
- `‚åòG / ‚áß‚åòG` ‚Äî Next / previous match in the search bar
- `‚åò+ / ‚åò=` ‚Äî Increase terminal font size
- `‚åò-` ‚Äî Decrease terminal font size
- `‚åò0` ‚Äî Reset terminal font size

### App
- `‚åòN` ‚Äî New terminal
- `‚åÉ‚åòB` ‚Äî Toggle sidebar visibility
- `‚åò,` ‚Äî Open Settings
- `‚áß‚åòB` ‚Äî Report a bug (bundles a screenshot, diagnostics, and recent activity)
- `‚åò/` ‚Äî Show the keyboard shortcut help
